title: Stub long-running plexus.evaluation.run with simulated streaming and budget
description:
# Stub long-running plexus.evaluation.run

## What

A stub for the canonical fan-out / dispatched operation that simulates:
- Items processed over time (realistic pacing — e.g., 1 item per ~50ms for a 1000-item evaluation = 50s total).
- Streaming progress events flowing back to the parent runtime through the trace bridge.
- Cost accumulation against the per-call budget envelope.
- Graceful halt with partial results when budget is exhausted mid-flight.
- Cancellation responding to a parent-side signal.

## Why a stub is sufficient for the spike

The spike validates the Lua-authoring hypothesis and the streaming/handle ergonomics. The actual distributed-budget infrastructure (Epic 4) is too large to build before the spike runs. A stub that simulates the externally-observable behavior is enough to validate the AGENT'S experience of using these operations, which is what the spike measures.

## Stubbed behavior

- Synchronous: `plexus.evaluation.run{ id = "X" }` blocks Lua, streams progress, returns final result.
- Async: `plexus.evaluation.run{ id = "X", async = true }` returns a handle immediately; `plexus.handle.await{ id = ... }` blocks for the result.
- Budget-aware: on every simulated item processed, debits a fixed simulated cost from ambient budget. Halts when exhausted.
- Streaming format: `{ event = "progress", items_done = N, items_total = M, current_metric = ..., cost_so_far = ... }` events emitted to the trace bridge.

## Deliverable

Extension of the PlexusModule stub plus a small simulated-budget implementation (just enough for the spike — NOT the real Epic 4 infrastructure).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress: implemented the long-running evaluation stub and handle namespace in `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py`.

Added:
- `plexus.evaluation.run{...}` synchronous mode with simulated per-item budget debits and progress events.
- `plexus.evaluation.run{ async = true }` returning a stable handle.
- `plexus.handle.{status,await,cancel}` for async-handle workflows.
- `PlexusModule.stream_events()` so the future harness can inspect streamed events.
- Budget-exhaustion path returns partial results with structured `BUDGET_EXHAUSTED` error data.

Validation run:
- `python -m py_compile spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py`
- Sync evaluation: completed 20 items with 5 progress events.
- Async evaluation: handle created, status read, awaited to completion.
- Cancellation: handle moved to `cancelled`.
- Low-budget run: halted with `budget_exhausted`, `partial=true`, processed 5 items.

Task remains `in_progress` pending review/landing.
